text: I promise .” Gary stared at her for a long while, finally nodding. “I trust you , Lara .” “Help me convince the others. The more people stand against Warlord, the better. We can’t allow him to do this anymore .” “Let me talk to her. Make sure she knows what’s happening .” “We’ll be at the water pump .” Lara met Peterson’s gaze as Gary went inside . “She’ll be in good hands,” the soldier said . “I know. Thank you .” The soldiers followed Lara as she wound through the slums, knocking on doors and calling to people through tent flaps. She roused some from their sleep, but many were already awake, preparing for the long, unforgiving day ahead . She told all of them to gather at the water pump. More than one person swore at her, and a few — especially as the light strengthened and the bits of red hair beneath her hood became clear — called her a traitor. Lara didn’t let it faze her; this was their one and only chance, and she’d see it through . Gary caught up with her before long, and people seemed more willing to listen to him. His loss was recent, but had been witnessed by many, and everyone had suffered somehow at the hands of the gearheads. They were everywhere — men and women missing fingers, or hands, or legs. Victims of justice . More homes than she remembered bore Warlord’s symbol on their doors . When they finally reached the common area around the water pump, a crowd had gathered, speaking to each other in hushed voices. Tension thickened the air . She found Newton and the other soldiers on the fringes of the crowd. He was wrapped in cloth from head to toe, his casing still bare . “Any sign of gearheads?” Lara asked . “No. Everything is, relatively, quiet .” “Won’t be for much longer,” Cooper said, gaze shifting to the northeast. “Best get on with this, so we can move when the time is right .” Lara brushed her sweat-dampened palms over her skirt and approached the crowd . We will make this work. We have to . “What’s this all about?” an older man demanded. Lara recognized his scowling face; Steve had been a fieldworker when she was young, one of the few humans allowed to toil under the watch of the gearheads, until a cut on his leg had become infected. He’d kept the leg, but never overcame his limp. “I could be out pickin’ already. We’re losin’ good time !” His voice carried over the other conversation, and attention swung toward Lara . “Some of us ain’t spreading our legs to bang bots for food.” Scarlet sneered, glaring at Lara with obvious envy. They were nearly the same age, but had never been close; Tabitha was Lara’s only real friend . Clamping her mouth shut, Lara closed her eyes. She wouldn’t allow herself to give in to anger. She needed these people on her side, on their own side. When she opened her eyes again, she ran her gaze over every person she could see, meeting their eyes one-by-one. They were scared, hungry, uncertain. The only difference between Lara and these people was that she’d learned to hope for something better . “I’m not going to lie to you. I left here with a bot. A dustwalker named Ronin. I did it because I was hungry, yeah, but more because I wanted to find my sister .” “Another bot-fucking whore.” Scarlet spat on the ground at Lara’s feet . “That’s enough!” Gary stepped forward. At the edges of her vision, Lara saw Cooper and his men closing in , too . “You gonna stand here and defend this filth?” Steve demanded . “Filth? Lara sacrificed to help my family when no one else would. She had nothing , and still gave to us. She traded good salvage for scraps of food, and we took it for granted. I’ll always regret that. But I’m standing beside her now…like I should’ve, all along .” “The bots she left with killed your children!” someone shouted . Gary’s face tightened, and he clenched his fists at his sides. “No. Warlord killed my children, and Lara is here to end him .” Humorless laughter spread through parts of the crowd . “She ain’t ending anything !” “Not by myself,” Lara replied . “You can’t expect us to fight him !” “Why? Why can’t we fight him? Because he’s put fear in our hearts?” Lara nodded to Cooper, and he stepped forward with his soldiers. They stood out despite the tattered coats and cloaks over their uniforms. “These people came to stand beside us, to join our fight against Warlord .” “Who the hell are they?” Steve asked . “Soldiers,” Lara said, turning back toward the crowd. “And this is only a handful of them .” “Why would they help us ?” “I asked them to .” Everyone was silent. They stared at Lara, stared at the soldiers. Conflict was clear on so many of their faces . “Warlord killed my sister and dismantled the bot who was caring for her because they broke his rules. Me and Ronin left before he could do the same to us. We risked the Dust to be together. Call me whatever you want, but Ronin is as human as any of us, and he’s mine . He may be built different, but he thinks. He feels . “And Warlord chased us down. Through a fucking dust storm. He chased us down, deactivated Ronin, and beat me near to death. He left me in the dirt to die, just so I’d suffer a little longer. That’s all he’s ever done. Our pain amuses him. Haven’t we all suffered enough ?” Murmured voices spread through the crowd. People shifted uncomfortably on their feet, staring at mutilated limbs, at neighbors who’d lost loved ones . “Newton, the bot who offered us
